title: Unit 15: The Genitive Case - Grammar Podcast

script.text: Unit 15 introduces the Genitive Case, the primary indicator of possession in Modern Greek. This case is the equivalent of the English 's' or the 'of' preposition. Athina, how is the possessive structure typically formulated?
Ο σκύλος του Γιώργου. Το σπίτι της Μαρίας.

Correct. We always utilize the structure: 'The Item' followed by 'of the Owner'. Regarding pluralisation, the definite article 'Ton' is used universally regardless of gender. Athina, please provide plural examples.
Των φίλων. Των γυναικών. Των παιδιών.
Regarding noun morphology: masculine nouns ending in '-os' typically change to '-ou', while feminines add an '-s'. Neuter nouns ending in '-ma' expand into '-matos'. Athina?
Το μάθημα – του μαθήματος. Το πρόβλημα – του προβλήματος.
It is also critical to note the accentual shift. If a word is stressed on the third syllable from the end, the Genitive case frequently forces the stress one syllable to the right. Athina?
Ο άνθρωπος – του ανθρώπου. Το θέατρο – του θεάτρου.

Lastly, the Genitive also expresses indirect objects and content relationships, as seen in the phrase 'A glass of water'. This concludes our B1 Intermediate review of Unit 15. Mastering the Genitive case is essential for articulating ownership and relationships in Modern Greek.
